heading: Obligations and rights under operating agreements
label: 53105 Obligations and rights under operating agreements

children.label: A
children.text: shall be operated exclusively in the foreign commerce or, except as provided in paragraph (2), in mixed foreign commerce and domestic trade allowed under a registry endorsement issued under ; andsection 12111 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: shall not otherwise be operated in the coastwise trade;

in the case of a vessel, other than a replacement vessel under subsection (f), first covered by an operating agreement after the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018, the vessel shall not be operated in the transportation of cargo between points in the United States and its territories either directly or via a foreign port; and
the vessel shall be documented under chapter 121 of this title.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An operating agreement under this chapter shall require, subject to the availability of appropriations, that the Secretary make a payment each fiscal year to the contractor in accordance with section 53106.

children.heading: .—Operating agreement is obligation of united states government
children.label: 2 .—Operating agreement is obligation of united states government
children.text: An operating agreement under this chapter constitutes a contractual obligation of the United States Government to pay the amounts provided for in the agreement to the extent of actual appropriations.

children.heading: .—Documentation Requirement
children.label: c .—Documentation Requirement
children.text: Each vessel covered by an operating agreement (including an agreement terminated under section 53104(c)(2)) shall remain documented under chapter 121 of this title, until the date the operating agreement would terminate according to its terms.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: A contractor with respect to an operating agreement (including an agreement terminated under section 53104(c)(2)) shall continue to be bound by the provisions of section 53107 until the date the operating agreement would terminate according to its terms.

children.heading: .—Emergency preparedness agreement
children.label: 2 .—Emergency preparedness agreement
children.text: All terms and conditions of an Emergency Preparedness Agreement entered into under section 53107 shall remain in effect until the date the operating agreement would terminate according to its terms, except that the terms of such Emergency Preparedness Agreement may be modified by the mutual consent of the contractor, the Secretary of Transportation, and the Secretary of Defense.

children.heading: .—Transfer of Operating Agreements
children.label: e .—Transfer of Operating Agreements
children.text: A contractor under an operating agreement may transfer the agreement (including all rights and obligations under the operating agreement) to any person that is eligible to enter into the operating agreement under this chapter if the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense determine that the transfer is in the best interests of the United States. A transaction shall not be considered a transfer of an operating agreement if the same legal entity with the same vessels remains the contracting party under the operating agreement.

children.heading: .—Replacement Vessels
children.label: f .—Replacement Vessels
children.text: A contractor may replace a vessel under an operating agreement with another vessel that is eligible to be included in the Fleet under section 53102(b), if the Secretary, in conjunction with the Secretary of Defense, approves the replacement of the vessel.
